title: Who Shot Mr Burns 1

jokes.text: Is there nothing so intoxicating as the school hallway at early morn?
jokes.setup: Principal Skinner's overly poetic appreciation of school
jokes.punchline: The absurdly romantic language about a mundane school hallway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - his pretentious poeticism about the most mundane aspects of school administration

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: The hallways of Springfield Elementary are like the corridors of my heart—lined with the lockers of memory, echoing with the footsteps of youth. Each bulletin board a canvas of dreams, each fluorescent light a beacon of knowledge guiding our precious students through the labyrinth of adolescence.

jokes.text: The school normally doesn't smell so rank.
jokes.setup: Following Skinner's romantic description of the school
jokes.punchline: Willie's blunt acknowledgment that the school normally stinks anyway
jokes.explanation: Good contrast to Skinner's romanticism, Willie's practical bluntness about school conditions

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: Aye, well it smells like feet and broken dreams most days anyway.

jokes.text: Poor fellow. Crushed by his own water bottle.
jokes.setup: Discovery of the dead gerbil
jokes.punchline: The absurd cause of death - killed by its own water bottle
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and absurd - the irony of being killed by your own water source

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: The gerbil is dead.

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: How did it die?

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: It was killed by its own water bottle.

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: That's the most absurd thing I've ever heard.

jokes.text: You're lucky you're getting a decent burial. Me own father got thrown in the bog.
jokes.setup: Being asked to give the gerbil a proper burial
jokes.punchline: Willie's casual revelation about his father's undignified death
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Willie humor - casual revelation of family tragedy while complaining about gerbil treatment

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: Aye, I'll give the wee beastie a proper burial. Me father didnae get one—he was ne'er found.

jokes.text: What in the name of Saint Ephesiocritus...?
jokes.setup: Willie's reaction to something shocking
jokes.punchline: Made-up saint name that sounds authentically Scottish/Catholic
jokes.explanation: Clever made-up saint name that feels authentic to Willie's character and dialect

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: Saint Och Aye the Noo! Mother of Mercy!

jokes.text: Smells like one of van Houten's. - It does not.
jokes.setup: Students trying to identify the smell
jokes.punchline: Immediate defensive response reveals it probably is Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Classic Milhouse moment - his quick defensive response confirms the accusation

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: What is that smell?

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: It's not me!

jokes.text: Pete Porter. Pass it on. Pasadena. Promptly. Package parcel processing. Perk up. Package of plant profit projection for Pete Porter in Pasadena. Priority? Precisely.
jokes.setup: Simple package delivery instruction
jokes.punchline: Escalating alliteration that becomes tongue-twisting absurdity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating alliteration sequence that becomes increasingly absurd and difficult to say

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Alright, listen up! We've got a package to deliver to Pismo Beach.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Pismo Beach? That's a pretty place.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Pretty Pismo Beach package placement problem, people!

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Practical Pismo Beach package placement problems persistently perplex postal personnel.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Perpetually problematic Pismo Beach package placement procedures plague particularly perplexed postal persons persistently procrastinating over persnickety parcel positioning protocols.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: P-P-P-P-PISMO BEACH!

jokes.text: My name is the return address, you senseless dunderpate.
jokes.setup: Employee saying 'Here's your package, Mr. Burns'
jokes.punchline: Burns' illogical anger and creative insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - irrational anger combined with his signature archaic insults

dialogue.speaker: Employee
dialogue.line: Here's your package, Mr. Burns.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: A package? I didn't order a package. Take it back!

dialogue.speaker: Employee
dialogue.line: But sir, you ordered it yourself last week.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Silence! You and your insolent package have wounded me deeply. Now get out, you sniveling ink-stained wretch!

jokes.text: My name is Homer J. Simp... ...son. Sounded large when I ordered it. I can't make hide nor hair of these metric booby traps.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to introduce himself to Burns
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by package size and confused by measurements
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - gets distracted mid-sentence by something completely unrelated, perfect ADD characterization

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns! I'd like to introduce myself. I'm Homer Simpson, and I—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, is that a package? How big is it?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Is that like, 12 inches? Or... what's bigger, a foot or a yard?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Because I'm pretty sure a yard is bigger than a foot, but that doesn't make sense.

jokes.text: Why is it when I heard the word 'school' and the word 'exploded' I thought of the word 'Skinner'?!
jokes.setup: Chalmers arriving at the exploded school
jokes.punchline: His immediate assumption that Skinner is somehow responsible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chalmers-Skinner dynamic - his immediate assumption of Skinner's culpability in any disaster

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: Skinner! What in blazes happened here?

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Superintendent Chalmers, I can explain—

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: I should have known you'd find a way to bungle this, Skinner. Incompetence of this magnitude is unmistakable!

jokes.text: An oil well doesn't belong in the hands of Betsy Bleeding-heart and Maynard G. Muskie-vote.
jokes.setup: Burns objecting to the school having oil money
jokes.punchline: Creative dismissive nicknames for liberals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns characterization - creative alliterative insults that capture his political views perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You want me to give money to the school? Bah! Hand it over to those bleeding-heart liberals? I'd sooner give it to a bunch of tree-hugging, Birkenstock-wearing, NPR-listening, Prius-driving, latte-sipping, coastal-elitist eggheads!

jokes.text: At the picnic, he thought my son Reynaldo was Rolando. Can you believe it?
jokes.setup: Consoling Homer about Burns not knowing names
jokes.punchline: Carl's own example shows Burns' name confusion isn't that bad
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Carl's attempt to console Homer actually shows very minor name confusion compared to complete anonymity

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Hey Homer, don't feel bad. Mr. Burns doesn't know my name either.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Yeah, he calls me 'Lenny.'

jokes.text: What's that say under your hand there? Oh, it's an unrelated article. It's an unrelated article. Within the banner headline.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to hide bad news in newspaper
jokes.punchline: Obvious attempt to cover up bad news that's clearly related
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward Skinner moment - obviously lying while being caught in the act

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: I'm sure no one will notice if I just... rearrange these newspaper clippings.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Principal Skinner, why does the headline say 'School Cafeteria Fire Contained' but you've pasted it next to 'Local Man Loses Entire Arm'?

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Context, my boy. It's all about context.

jokes.text: I want a crystal bucket for my slop water. And a brand-new filthy blanket.
jokes.setup: Willie making budget requests for the oil money
jokes.punchline: Absurd combination of luxury item with deliberately dirty item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie logic - wanting luxury for mundane purposes while specifically requesting a 'brand-new filthy' blanket

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: Aye, and with the oil money, I'll be needin' a solid gold toilet seat... for me regular toilet that I never clean!

jokes.text: Tito Puente! Tito Puente!
jokes.setup: Introduction of jazz program instructor
jokes.punchline: Genuine excitement for a real jazz legend in elementary school
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Lisa's jazz obsession and the absurdity of having a real legend teach elementary school

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: A real jazz instructor? At Springfield Elementary?

dialogue.speaker: Students
dialogue.line: Yeah! This is so cool!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Finally, someone who understands the true art form. I can't believe they brought in an actual jazz legend to teach us.

dialogue.speaker: Students
dialogue.line: This is the best day ever!

jokes.text: That's odd. I don't have a secretary. Or an intercom. But send them in.
jokes.setup: Secretary announcing a visitor over intercom
jokes.punchline: Skinner's realization he has neither secretary nor intercom but accepts it anyway
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Skinner recognizes the impossibility but goes along with it anyway

dialogue.speaker: Secretary
dialogue.line: Principal Skinner, you have a visitor.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: But I don't have a secretary or an intercom.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, I'll accept it anyway.

jokes.text: Ahoy there, dean. I understand you're taking suggestions from students, eh? Well, me and my fourth-form chums think it would be quite corking if you'd sign over your oil well to the local energy concern.
jokes.setup: Burns disguising himself as a student
jokes.punchline: Transparent disguise with archaic language completely giving him away
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - completely transparent disguise using language no student would ever use

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Ah yes, I am certainly a student. See my blue jeans and... radical leather jacket. Cowabunga, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns? Is that you?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Nonsense! I am but a mere youth of the contemporary era. Verily, I shall now attend to my studies with great alacrity.

jokes.text: I own the electric company, waterworks, plus the hotel on Baltic Avenue.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming to have a monopoly
jokes.punchline: Comparing his real empire to Monopoly board game properties
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that makes Burns' monopolistic tendencies both more relatable and absurd

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: I don't play Monopoly. I own the actual properties the game is based on.

jokes.text: That hotel's a dump and your monopoly's pathetic.
jokes.setup: Burns' boasting about his Monopoly properties
jokes.punchline: Skinner's surprisingly harsh comeback about the worst property
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Monopoly reference with Skinner showing unexpected backbone

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Yeah, well at least I don't own Baltic Avenue. That's just sad, Mr. Burns.

jokes.text: Sorry, sir. This was all I could find. Take that. And that.
jokes.setup: Burns asking Smithers to help subdue Skinner
jokes.punchline: Smithers' pathetic attempt at violence with inadequate weapon
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Smithers' incompetence at anything requiring aggression

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, help me subdue Principal Skinner!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Yes, sir!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Take this, you fiend!

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Ow! You're hitting me with a limp noodle!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: It's all I could find on short notice, sir!

jokes.text: Please don't waste those.
jokes.setup: Being attacked with supplies by Smithers
jokes.punchline: Skinner's practical concern about wasting school supplies mid-attack
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - even while being attacked, he's concerned about waste and fiscal responsibility

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Smithers, please! You're wasting valuable school supplies!

jokes.text: I guess they'd wanna be with their families or something, huh?
jokes.setup: Being told the Three Stooges wouldn't hang out with him if resurrected
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent assumption about their priorities
jokes.explanation: Sweet Bart moment showing his childlike logic about celebrity priorities

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if I had a time machine, I could go back and...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, they'd probably just want to do their old routines. I bet they wouldn't even notice me.

jokes.text: That's it. Frimble about with your widgets and doobobs.
jokes.setup: Homer getting angry at work
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical technical vocabulary when frustrated
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - making up technical terms that sound vaguely mechanical

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why you little—blammy-whammy-zippity-doo! The doohickey isn't synergizing with the thingamabob!

jokes.text: Say, that sounds like a 'larf.' Let's try it right now.
jokes.setup: Burns saying 'taking candy from a baby'
jokes.punchline: Burns' excited decision to literally try taking candy from a baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns evil innocence - taking a metaphor literally and being excited about the cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, I'm going to try taking candy from a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: But sir, that's just an expression.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Is it? Well, I'm going anyway!

jokes.text: There's candy right here. Why don't we eat this instead of stealing.
jokes.setup: Burns wanting to steal candy from a baby
jokes.punchline: Smithers' practical suggestion to avoid baby theft
jokes.explanation: Good Smithers moment - trying to redirect Burns' evil impulses toward less problematic actions

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, I want that baby's candy. How do we steal it without getting caught?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Well sir, perhaps you could simply ask the baby for the candy.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Fool! Where's the fun in that?

jokes.text: Only the sour quince log, sir. Dispose of it.
jokes.setup: Going through candy box
jokes.punchline: The most unappetizing candy name possible
jokes.explanation: Good fake product name that sounds genuinely unappetizing

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Sir, I've arranged the candies by color and flavor.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Excellent, Smithers. What do we have here?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Lemon drops, butterscotch, and... uh... these are called Ass Blasters.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Ass Blasters? That's the most unappetizing candy name possible!

jokes.text: Nerd!
jokes.setup: Lisa being introduced as top student
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile reaction to academic achievement
jokes.explanation: Standard Lisa nerd moment - predictable but fits the show's pattern

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Thank you. I'm very honored to be recognized as the top student.

dialogue.speaker: Student 1
dialogue.line: Nerd!

dialogue.speaker: Student 2
dialogue.line: Geek!

dialogue.speaker: Multiple Students
dialogue.line: Loser! Freak! Weirdo!

jokes.text: There's no pressure. Someone else has tapped this well.
jokes.setup: Ceremonial first activation of oil pump
jokes.punchline: Immediate discovery that Burns has stolen their oil
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony moment - the ceremony immediately becomes a disaster

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: Well, laddie, let's get this bonnie pump up and runnin'!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Ah yes, wonderful. Though I should mention, I've already been siphoning your oil for the past three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: What?! Ye thievin' scoundrel!

jokes.text: Almost sexual, isn't it, Smithers?
jokes.setup: Watching oil pump apparatus
jokes.punchline: Burns' inappropriate sexualization of industrial equipment
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns creepy moment - inappropriate commentary on machinery

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, notice how the piston moves back and forth. Quite hypnotic... and sensual.

jokes.text: I almost forgot. Wouldn't want you gnawing on those casts, eh, boy?
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper getting medical treatment
jokes.punchline: Dog cone of shame to prevent cast chewing
jokes.explanation: Standard pet medical humor - the cone of shame is a reliable visual gag

dialogue.speaker: Veterinarian
dialogue.line: I'm putting a cone on Santa's Little Helper to prevent him from chewing on his cast.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A cone? What is he, an ice cream cone?

dialogue.speaker: Veterinarian
dialogue.line: It's called the cone of shame. Dogs hate wearing them.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: The cone of shame? That's hilarious!

jokes.text: And wound that Mr. Smithers. Out of my way.
jokes.setup: Willie's rage at losing his job
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific plan to only wound Smithers, not kill him
jokes.explanation: Great Willie logic - his proportional response plan shows thoughtful violence planning

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: I'll tie Smithers to the cannon and fire him into the sun! No wait, that'll kill him. I'll fire him into the *moon*. That way he'll just float around up there, slowly going mad!

jokes.text: Dad, this doesn't have your name on it.
jokes.setup: Homer showing Burns' thank you card as evidence Burns isn't bad
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out the card completely excludes Homer
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Homer's rage - Lisa's innocent observation destroys his defense of Burns

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, that card doesn't say anything about you. It just says 'Thanks for the extra effort, Simpson.'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly! See? He thanked me!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, he didn't even use your first name.

jokes.text: Dear Lord. That's the loudest profanity I've ever heard.
jokes.setup: Homer's off-screen profanity explosion
jokes.punchline: Ned's shocked reaction to unheard swearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned reaction and brilliant way to convey Homer's rage without showing the profanity

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Oh my stars! Such language! I haven't heard words like that since the war!

jokes.text: Sure, I'm all dizzy and nauseous but where's the inflated sense of self-esteem?
jokes.setup: Barney inhaling toxic fumes instead of drinking
jokes.punchline: His disappointment at not getting alcohol's psychological benefits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Barney moment - sophisticated analysis of alcohol's effects while desperately seeking any intoxication

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Mmm... toxic fumes. Wait, where's the buzz? Where's the emotional numbness? This isn't working at all!

jokes.text: Now, there's the inflated sense of self-esteem.
jokes.setup: Barney finally getting the psychological effect he wanted
jokes.punchline: Recognition when he achieves the desired mental state
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the earlier setup - Barney achieving his goal

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Finally! I've achieved the mental state I've always wanted!

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: What mental state is that, Barney?

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Unconsciousness.

jokes.text: I call this enemy... the sun.
jokes.setup: Burns discussing his greatest nemesis
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reveal that he considers the sun his enemy
jokes.explanation: Iconic Burns moment - perfectly absurd escalation of his evil schemes to literally fighting the sun

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: My greatest nemesis... the one foe I cannot defeat... the sun.

jokes.text: Since the beginning of time, man has yearned to destroy the sun. I will do the next best thing: Block it out.
jokes.setup: Burns' plan to block out the sun
jokes.punchline: His false historical claim about humanity's relationship with the sun
jokes.explanation: Legendary Burns quote - completely absurd historical revisionism to justify his insane plan

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You know, I'd like to thank you all for coming. Ever since the beginning of time, man has yearned to destroy the sun.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Sir, the sun has never threatened you.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: No, Smithers. When I was a boy, the sun came right up to the nipple on my factory and I said 'No more.'

jokes.text: Owls will deafen us with incessant hooting. The sundial will be useless.
jokes.setup: Smithers listing consequences of blocking out the sun
jokes.punchline: Escalating from serious to absurdly mundane concerns
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from ecological disaster to minor inconvenience - the sundial concern is perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Without the sun, Springfield will be plunged into darkness. All plant life will wither and die. The town will be engulfed in eternal night.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Our bodies will lose all sense of day and night. We'll be confused, disoriented, unable to tell when to sleep or wake.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: The temperature will plummet. We'll freeze solid within hours.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: And worst of all... Mr. Burns' sundial will be rendered completely useless.

jokes.text: No. No, Monty, I won't. Not until you step back from the brink of insanity.
jokes.setup: Smithers finally standing up to Burns
jokes.punchline: His dramatic phrasing of Burns' obvious insanity
jokes.explanation: Major character moment - Smithers finally drawing a line at blocking out the sun

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns, I can no longer stand by and watch you destroy this company with your reckless decisions and complete disconnection from reality.

dialogue.speaker: Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, are you having some sort of episode?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: No sir. I'm finally seeing things clearly. You're not just eccentric, sir. You're clinically insane.

dialogue.speaker: Burns
dialogue.line: Insane? Why, that's the most preposterous thing I've ever heard. Now get me my breakfast of raw meat and coffee made from the tears of my enemies.

jokes.text: There has been a shocking decline in the quality and quantity of your toadying.
jokes.setup: Burns firing Smithers for insubordination
jokes.punchline: His formal evaluation of Smithers' sycophantic performance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns dialogue - treating sycophancy as a measurable job performance metric. The formal language makes it even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, you're fired for insubordination.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: But sir, I've devoted my entire life to serving you!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, and you've done a terrible job. My formal evaluation: completely inadequate sycophancy. You should have been groveling at my feet by now. Instead, you merely shuffle about like some common toady. Now get out before I have security remove you.

jokes.text: Take that, Bowl-a-rama. Take that, convenience mart. Take that, nuclear power pla... Oh, fiddlesticks.
jokes.setup: Grandpa attacking various establishments
jokes.punchline: Accidentally attacking his own son's workplace and mild reaction
jokes.explanation: Good Grandpa moment - his mild reaction to accidentally targeting the nuclear plant

dialogue.speaker: Grandpa Simpson
dialogue.line: Why, I'll teach you to mess with me!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Dad, that's the Nuclear Power Plant!

dialogue.speaker: Grandpa Simpson
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, carry on then.

jokes.text: Sorry, Grampa. It's just, for a second, it looked like Dad had melted.
jokes.setup: Bart not responding to Grandpa
jokes.punchline: Explanation that Homer appeared to have melted
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup - Homer's slumped position making him look melted

dialogue.speaker: Grandpa Simpson
dialogue.line: Bart! Bart, wake up!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What? I'm awake.

dialogue.speaker: Grandpa Simpson
dialogue.line: Your father's in the living room. He appears to have melted.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Melted? But it's not even that hot...

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Smithers, who is that ignoramus? Smithers, who is that lollygagger? Who is that blubber-pot? Who is that bafflewit? Lummox. Puddinghead. Mooncalf. Limpnoodle. Goldbricker. Drizzlepuss...
jokes.setup: Burns repeatedly asking about Homer while not remembering his name
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of creative archaic insults
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating insult sequence - perfect Burns vocabulary getting increasingly elaborate

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Who is that lummox in Sector 7G?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: That's Homer Simpson, sir. One of your employees.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Simpson? Bah! That bumbling nincompoop has cost me thousands!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Yes, sir. Shall I have him terminated?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: No, no. But that knave, that rapscallion, that absolute buffoon will answer to me!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Right away, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: And tell that dunderheaded miscreant, that lily-livered blackguard, that preposterous oaf that his days of incompetence are numbered!

jokes.text: Stop it. Stop it. Stop it!
jokes.setup: Burns' endless stream of insults
jokes.punchline: Homer finally snapping and yelling
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the insult barrage - Homer reaching his breaking point

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's it! I've had it with you and your insults, Burns! You think you're so great, but you're just a withered old prune who can't remember anyone's name!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Why, you impudent fool! Security!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, shut up! I'm done! You can take this job and—

jokes.text: Oh, you're a dead man, Burns. Oh, you're dead. You're dead, Burns!
jokes.setup: Homer finally introducing himself properly
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatening murder after stating his name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's immediate escalation from introduction to death threats

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hello, I'm Homer Simpson.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Now I'm gonna kill you!

jokes.text: Hey, the lamp's running away. That's my dog, man.
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper in cone and wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Lenny mistaking the medical equipment for a lamp
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag - the cone and wheelchair setup making the dog look like a mobile lamp

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Hey Homer, nice lamp. Where'd you get it?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's not a lamp, Lenny. That's Santa's Little Helper. He's in a cone and wheelchair.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, it'd make a great lamp.

jokes.text: How can you have a house without a gun? What if a bear came through the door?
jokes.setup: Marge taking away the gun
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's absurd but earnest concern about bears
jokes.explanation: Classic Grandpa paranoia - his specific and irrational fear of bear home invasion

dialogue.speaker: Grandpa Simpson
dialogue.line: But what about bears? I've been wanting to shoot a bear in the butt my whole life!

jokes.text: You should've fired into the air. She would've run off.
jokes.setup: Marge burying the gun to keep it from children
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's tactical advice about the hypothetical bear
jokes.explanation: Good followup to the bear concern - Grandpa's specific tactical knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Grandpa Simpson
dialogue.line: If a bear attacks, go for the gun buried in the yard. It's like I always say: 'An armed society is a polite society.' Well, unless the bear has a gun. Then you're just two heavily armed bears fighting over territory.

jokes.text: Oh, dear God. It's not that bad. I mean... I never miss Pardon My Zinger.
jokes.setup: Smithers describing his post-firing depression
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to defend his TV viewing habits
jokes.explanation: Good Smithers depression moment - defending his lowbrow TV consumption

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: I've been watching so much television lately. It's educational! I learned that the plural of 'mongoose' is 'mongeese'.

jokes.text: Those wheels are squeaking a bit. Perhaps I could sell him a little oil.
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper in wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Burns seeing business opportunity in disabled dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns evil - seeing profit opportunity in a disabled dog's medical equipment

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: A dog in a wheelchair? Why, that's the perfect spokes-animal for my new line of disabled-accessible luxury goods!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Sir, I don't think that's appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Quiet, Smithers. I can already see the profits rolling in. We'll call it 'Burns Mobility Solutions.' The crippled pooch is our mascot.

jokes.text: I'd like to hear from Sideshow Mel.
jokes.setup: Town meeting about Burns
jokes.punchline: Moe wanting to hear from the most articulate person
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment - Moe recognizing Sideshow Mel's eloquence

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: I'd like to hear from the most articulate person here.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: I'll see that Mr. Burns suffers the infernal machinations of hell's grim tyrant.
jokes.setup: Moe wanting to hear from the articulate Sideshow Mel
jokes.punchline: Mel's extremely overwrought threat against Burns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sideshow Mel - overly theatrical language to express simple violent intent

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Hey Mel, say something for us, would ya?

dialogue.speaker: Sideshow Mel
dialogue.line: Certainly. If Mr. Burns were to cross me, I would first remove his skin and use it to make a nice wallet. Then I'd break his bones one by one, slowly, methodically. I'd feed his organs to my trained hyenas, keeping only his heart for myself so I could crush it in my bare hands while he watches. And then—oh, the things I would do to him would make the Inquisition look like a gentle massage.

jokes.text: Once, when I was sailing around the Arctic... - Shut up, you.
jokes.setup: Burns asking if anyone has seen 3pm sunset
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain starting story immediately shut down by Burns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns impatience with legitimate answer to his rhetorical question. Sea Captain's expertise being dismissed is good character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Has anyone seen a 3pm sunset?

dialogue.speaker: Sea Captain
dialogue.line: Ar, that reminds me of the time I was sailin' through the straits of...

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: What the...? Eternal darkness. Well, that's just great.
jokes.setup: Snake returning to perpetual twilight
jokes.punchline: His understated reaction to apocalyptic change
jokes.explanation: Good Snake moment - his casual reaction to the sun being blocked out

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Dude... that's cool.

jokes.text: Sorry, I was in the can.
jokes.setup: Someone asking where a gun-toting lowlife is when needed
jokes.punchline: Snake's casual explanation for missing the action
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke - Snake's mundane explanation for missing the call to action

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: I was in the bathroom, man. I get all plugged up from the chili.

jokes.text: Hello, lamppost. What you knowing? I've come to watch your power flowing.
jokes.setup: Burns admiring his electric monopoly
jokes.punchline: Singing parody of Simon & Garfunkel while talking to streetlight
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference moment - Burns adapting folk song to celebrate his evil plan

jokes.text: Oh, wait, it is Burns. His wrinkly skin looks like a mask.
jokes.setup: Wiggum thinking Burns' face is a mask
jokes.punchline: Realization that Burns just looks that artificial naturally
jokes.explanation: Good Burns physical comedy - his appearance is so unnatural it seems fake

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Sir, I'm gonna have to ask you to remove that mask.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: This is my face.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

jokes.text: Well, I couldn't possibly solve this mystery. Can you?
jokes.setup: Wiggum being asked to solve the shooting
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to ask audience to solve it
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta moment - Wiggum's incompetence leading to direct audience address

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well, we've got a real mystery on our hands here. So, uh, if anybody out there in TV land has any information about this shooting, please call the Springfield Police Department. We're stumped.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'll give it a shot. I mean, you know, it's my job, right?
jokes.setup: Being asked to solve the shooting mystery
jokes.punchline: Accidental pun about 'giving it a shot' in shooting case
jokes.explanation: Subtle wordplay - Lou's unintentional pun about shooting in a shooting case

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: Yeah, we'll give it a shot.

tmdb_overview: Mr. Burns makes many enemies when he steals the oil discovered beneath Springfield Elementary.
insight: Season 6's finale hits 90.8 with nearly three jokes per minute across 59 gags.
